pages.title: Axialent Leadership Workshop on January 28th
pages.description: Thank you for your participation and engagement in the workshop. We invite you to evaluate the experience and give us feedback. We value your opinion, which will help us improve.

headings.heading:
Consider the score from 1 to 5.
1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree.

rows.text: a. This workshop is contributing in a positive way to my life
b. How likely are you to recommend others to attend to this workshop?
c. The workshop taught me actionable skills, techniques or approaches that I will use
d. My understanding of the workshop topics increased

headings.heading:
Consider the score from 1 to 5, with 5 being the highest score.
The facilitator...

rows.text: a. demonstrates robust knowledge of the workshop content.
b. creates an environment of trust in which we can address important matters for our team.
c. generates and facilitates discussions that help understand the proposed methodology.

headings.heading: What are the 3 most positive aspects of the workshop session for you?
What do you think would be appropriate to modify to improve its effectiveness?
Is there anything we can help you with to develop your personal and your Executive Leadership Team?

answers.text: Helped us get to know each other better
More interactive sessions rather than slide based presentations

The insight from the exercises
The openness from the team
The realisation we can have powerful results quickly

Whilst needed the intro felt long but i understand why
Have some rules about the fishbowl - i was astounded we lost PM immediately 
More movement exercises
I hope we move forward together for next steps - look forward to discussing
Well done on holding PM accountable - powerful

Reminders on straightforward communication
Facilitator calling out even the senior execs if not fully present
PartnerRe ELT getting the joint messages so we can show a consistent leadership to the group
A bit more depth and discussions of real life situations and how to best handle them
More of these sessions with direct follow-ups.

Energizing
Awareness about my peers
Relevant tools
Schedule more time
Practice in workshops
Thank you Anabel!

I/We/It Model - reflection
Understanding of underlying Mindset topic
concrete / actionable "follow ups" - ARR
More exercises
NA
Revisiting mindset / pragmatic thinking / creating a sense of shared leadership across the ELT
More time
Not at the moment
It was an impactful session.  I hope we can continue to build on the themes in future sessions and take the learnings into our daily work at PartnerRe
1. good balance of theory and practice 2. first open/uncomfortable conversation amongst the ELT 3. nil
Maybe  more time on the final exercise.
Some thought/techniques on how to stay connected with a distributed/global leadership team.
I thoroughly enjoyed the session.
1) Interactive nature of workshop, 2) use of analogies (makes concepts easy to remember), 3) I, It, We framework
More time (possibly broken up) - we covered a lot of ground in a short period of time.
direct application of theory
none
none
none
Victim as opposed to player mindset, the left side as opposed to the right side of the sheet.
To apply the principles more concretely to our collective modus operandi in the ELT.

-generated positive conversation
-common language and context to talk about leadership and conversations
-focus on personal accountability
nothing
doesn't come to mind

raise awareness on self vs others
shift in mindset requires a lot of practice
the toxicity/left hand side of the conversation was/is an eye opener
provide tools for how to practice/flex so that it becomes a muscle

good content and good link between the frameworks with strong focus on application.
good energy and focus from the presenter
adapted to group dynamically
intro of the session felt long in hind-sight. all would have traded 20mins more at the end for 20mins less at the beginning  i suspect.
